rootCauseAnalysis.summary: The data-processor sidecar container in data-service executes a deliberate infinite loop writing 1 MB of random data every 5 seconds to a RAM-backed emptyDir volume, causing unbounded memory growth that is predicted to exhaust the container's 192 MiB memory limit imminently.
causalChain: Signal: ContainerMemoryExhaustionPredicted — container 'app' in demo-telemetry/data-service-6bfb5d698f-tctdb predicted to exceed memory limit within 30 minutes
Why? The pod's overall memory working set is growing at ~90 KB/s — driven by the data-processor sidecar, not the app container
Why? The data-processor container executes an infinite loop writing 1 MB of random data every 5 seconds to /data (a RAM-backed emptyDir tmpfs mount)
Why? The emptyDir volume is configured with medium: Memory, causing all writes to consume RAM rather than disk storage — directly inflating the container working set

Root cause: The Deployment spec for data-service configures data-processor with a memory-leaking command (unbounded writes to a RAM-backed volume) and insufficient memory limit (192 MiB) to sustain the workload indefinitely — the container will be OOM-killed as memory is exhausted

dueDiligence.targetAccuracy: Targeting Deployment/data-service because its spec.template.spec.containers[data-processor].args and spec.template.spec.volumes[data-cache] configuration are the source of the problem. Modifying the Deployment (fixing the command, changing volume medium, or adding cleanup) directly resolves the issue.
dueDiligence.proportionality: Single Deployment with 2 replicas affected. The fix is targeted: modify the data-processor container command or change volume medium in the Deployment spec. No other workloads are involved.
dueDiligence.scopeCompleteness: Investigated: both replica pods, Deployment spec, ReplicaSet, events for pod and Deployment, logs for app and data-processor containers in both pods, Prometheus memory metrics (WSS, limits, OOM events, growth rate). Checked node assignment (dev-worker-0 and dev-worker-1). No upstream/downstream dependencies are relevant to this memory exhaustion pattern.
dueDiligence.causalCompleteness: Traced from the alert signal → pod memory growth → data-processor sidecar behavior → RAM-backed emptyDir → Deployment spec misconfiguration. The root cause is the Deployment's container command and volume configuration. Cannot go deeper without application source code intent (this appears intentional/simulated).
dueDiligence.evidenceSufficiency: All claims are backed by tool output: kubectl_describe confirmed the infinite-write command and RAM-backed volume config; Prometheus confirmed data-processor WSS at 87.8% of 192 MiB limit with ~90 KB/s growth rate; container_oom_events_total confirmed no OOM has occurred yet; range query confirmed linear memory growth pattern from 0 to 23.4 MiB in ~2 minutes post-start.
dueDiligence.regressionAwareness: No prior remediation history available for Deployment/data-service in demo-telemetry (confirmed via get_namespaced_resource_context: tier1 and tier2 history both empty).
dueDiligence.alternativeHypotheses: Considered: (1) Node memory pressure — ruled out: no node-level events or pressure conditions observed, pods are spread across two separate nodes both showing same pattern; (2) app container memory leak — ruled out: app container WSS is only ~4.7 MiB against 64 MiB limit, stable; (3) External memory pressure from other workloads — ruled out: only 2 pods in demo-telemetry namespace, both owned by data-service.
dueDiligence.confidenceCalibration: 0.95: Starting at 1.0. -0.03 for the alert naming 'app' container while the actual driver is 'data-processor' (alert may be using pod-level aggregation); -0.02 for not being able to verify whether this is an intentional test workload or a genuine misconfiguration (the command pattern strongly suggests a simulated leak scenario).

contributingFactors: RAM-backed emptyDir volume (medium: Memory) causes tmpfs writes to count against container working set memory, not just disk
data-processor container memory limit (192 MiB) is 6x its request (32 MiB), creating a large burst window before enforcement
Both replica pods are affected identically — both are at ~87-88% of their memory limit
The emptyDir sizeLimit of 256 MiB exceeds the data-processor container limit of 192 MiB, meaning the container OOM kill will trigger before the volume limit enforcement
No memory growth ceiling or cleanup logic exists in the data-processor command

analysis.rootCauseSummary: The data-processor sidecar container in data-service executes a deliberate infinite loop writing 1 MB of random data every 5 seconds to a RAM-backed emptyDir volume, causing unbounded memory growth that is predicted to exhaust the container's 192 MiB memory limit imminently.

done

kaResponse.analysis: The data-processor sidecar container in data-service executes a deliberate infinite loop writing 1 MB of random data every 5 seconds to a RAM-backed emptyDir volume (medium: Memory). Because the volume uses tmpfs, all writes count toward the container's memory working set. Both replica pods are at ~87-88% of the 192 MiB container limit (~23 MiB headroom remaining), with growth at ~90 KB/s — exhaustion is predicted within ~4 minutes. No OOM events have occurred yet; this is a predicted exhaustion requiring immediate proactive intervention.

rootCauseAnalysis.summary: The data-processor sidecar container in data-service executes a deliberate infinite loop writing 1 MB of random data every 5 seconds to a RAM-backed emptyDir volume (medium: Memory). Because the volume uses tmpfs, all writes count toward the container's memory working set. Both replica pods are at ~87-88% of the 192 MiB container limit (~23 MiB headroom remaining), with growth at ~90 KB/s — exhaustion is predicted within ~4 minutes. No OOM events have occurred yet; this is a predicted exhaustion requiring immediate proactive intervention.
causalChain: Signal: ContainerMemoryExhaustionPredicted — container 'app' in demo-telemetry/data-service-6bfb5d698f-tctdb predicted to exceed memory limit within 30 minutes
Why? The pod's overall memory working set is growing at ~90 KB/s — driven by the data-processor sidecar, not the app container
Why? The data-processor container executes an infinite loop writing 1 MB of random data every 5 seconds to /data (a RAM-backed emptyDir tmpfs mount)
Why? The emptyDir volume is configured with medium: Memory, causing all writes to consume RAM rather than disk storage — directly inflating the container working set

Root cause: The Deployment spec for data-service configures data-processor with a memory-leaking command (unbounded writes to a RAM-backed volume) and insufficient memory limit (192 MiB) to sustain the workload indefinitely — the container will be OOM-killed as memory is exhausted

dueDiligence.targetAccuracy: Targeting Deployment/data-service because its spec.template.spec.containers[data-processor].args and spec.template.spec.volumes[data-cache] configuration are the source of the problem. Modifying the Deployment (fixing the command, changing volume medium, or adding cleanup) directly resolves the issue.
dueDiligence.proportionality: Single Deployment with 2 replicas affected. The fix is targeted: modify the data-processor container command or change volume medium in the Deployment spec. No other workloads are involved.
dueDiligence.scopeCompleteness: Investigated: both replica pods, Deployment spec, ReplicaSet, events for pod and Deployment, logs for app and data-processor containers in both pods, Prometheus memory metrics (WSS, limits, OOM events, growth rate). Checked node assignment (dev-worker-0 and dev-worker-1). No upstream/downstream dependencies are relevant to this memory exhaustion pattern.
dueDiligence.causalCompleteness: Traced from the alert signal → pod memory growth → data-processor sidecar behavior → RAM-backed emptyDir → Deployment spec misconfiguration. The root cause is the Deployment's container command and volume configuration. Cannot go deeper without application source code intent (this appears intentional/simulated).
dueDiligence.evidenceSufficiency: All claims are backed by tool output: kubectl_describe confirmed the infinite-write command and RAM-backed volume config; Prometheus confirmed data-processor WSS at 87.8% of 192 MiB limit with ~90 KB/s growth rate; container_oom_events_total confirmed no OOM has occurred yet; range query confirmed linear memory growth pattern from 0 to 23.4 MiB in ~2 minutes post-start.
dueDiligence.regressionAwareness: No prior remediation history available for Deployment/data-service in demo-telemetry (confirmed via get_namespaced_resource_context: tier1 and tier2 history both empty).
dueDiligence.alternativeHypotheses: Considered: (1) Node memory pressure — ruled out: no node-level events or pressure conditions observed, pods are spread across two separate nodes both showing same pattern; (2) app container memory leak — ruled out: app container WSS is only ~4.7 MiB against 64 MiB limit, stable; (3) External memory pressure from other workloads — ruled out: only 2 pods in demo-telemetry namespace, both owned by data-service.

contributingFactors: RAM-backed emptyDir volume (medium: Memory) causes tmpfs writes to count against container working set memory, not just disk
data-processor container memory limit (192 MiB) is 6x its request (32 MiB), creating a large burst window before enforcement
